event: A mother protecting her children in rising floodwaters
symbols: rising floodwaters — the overwhelming forces of nature or adversity threatening the vulnerable
mother clutching children — sacrificial maternal love and the instinct to protect the innocent
broken wooden fence — the inadequacy of human structures against the power of nature or divine judgment
distant boats or debris — the broader devastation surrounding the intimate human drama
description: A dramatic painting in warm, muted tones showing a young mother standing at the edge of rising floodwaters, clutching an infant to her chest with one arm while a small child clings to her skirts. She stands near a broken wooden fence or post that juts out of the swirling brown water. Her expression is one of alarm and determination as she looks out over the flood. The background shows a hazy, flooded landscape with indistinct structures partially submerged. The palette is dominated by warm browns, grays, and muted greens.

_meta.title: Interior of St Peter's Rome, Looking west
